title: resolvePromptField heuristic misclassifies inline prompts containing '/' as template file paths
description: resolvePromptField at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/agent-spec.ts line 21 classifies any single-line string containing a forward slash as a template file path: `value.endsWith('.md') || value.endsWith('.txt') || (value.includes('/') && !value.includes('\n'))`. Inline prompts that mention paths or URLs in natural language (e.g. 'Read docs/03-solar-engine.md') are routed into the template branch and stored in systemPromptTemplate / taskPromptTemplate instead of systemPrompt / taskPrompt. compileAgent later attempts to read the prompt text as a file path and fails with ENOENT. There is no explicit `inline:` discriminator parallel to the explicit `template:` discriminator in the YAML shape; classification is heuristic-only and the heuristic over-fires on natural-language strings.

evidence.reference: FB-001 'resolvePromptField heuristic misclassifies inline prompts as template file paths'; actual_behavior: prompt text 'Read docs/03-solar-engine.md' is stored as taskPromptTemplate instead of taskPrompt, compileAgent then renderTemplateFile fails with ENOENT

Investigation report FB-001 section confirms code at agent-spec.ts:21 verbatim; verdict 'Code + Schema (dual-layer fix)' — narrow resolvePromptField to treat value as template only if ends with .md or .txt, remove the includes('/') branch; schema introduces explicit inline vs template discriminator
resolvePromptField function definition with the over-firing heuristic on line 21

impact: Agent authors writing natural-language prompts that mention paths or URLs file specs that pass author-agent-spec validation, persist to the substrate, and fail at dispatch-time with ENOENT. The misclassification is silent at author-time and load-bearing at dispatch-time. Compounds with FGAP for FB-002 (YAML serializer collapses newlines, removing the only existing escape from the heuristic) and FGAP for FB-003 (round-trip validation does not catch the misclassification).
proposed_resolution: Code at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/agent-spec.ts line 21: replace the disjunction `value.endsWith('.md') || value.endsWith('.txt') || (value.includes('/') && !value.includes('\n'))` with `value.endsWith('.md') || value.endsWith('.txt')` (file-extension check only). Schema layer: introduce an explicit prompt-discriminator shape (either nested `{inline: string} | {template: string}` form, or sibling marker `systemPromptType: 'inline' | 'template'`) and document it in the AgentSpec schema.
